She was a pretty girl, but she was born into a family of clerks by mistake. She had no money for marriage and wanted to be loved by a rich man. But she married a small clerk at the Ministry of Public Instruction instead. She dressed simply because she felt unhappy and lost in the world. In society, women's beauty and charm matter more than family or rank. Natural beauty and elegance help women feel equal to others and show true worth. She always felt pain and wanted nice things because she wished for comfort in her life. She had problems because her home was poor and the furniture was old and ugly. Many things made her angry, but other women did not notice them at all. The little Breton peasant made her feel sad because she remembered her regrets and lost dreams. She thought of the quiet rooms with beautiful tapestries and bright candle holders. The footmen wore knee-length pants and slept in big chairs because they felt sleepy. The heat from the stove made them warm and drowsy. She thought of long rooms with silk and beautiful furniture, and she remembered special decorations. She imagined pretty rooms where friends talked, and many women wanted the attention of famous men. She sat for dinner at the round table with her husband, and he uncovered the soup. She thought about nice dinners with shining silverware and tapestries on the walls. The tapestries showed old people and strange birds in a magical forest. She imagined tasty dishes on beautiful plates and sweet words spoken softly while she ate. She enjoyed eating pink trout and quail wings. She had no dresses and no jewels. She had nothing at all. She loved only that, and she felt made for it. She wanted people to like her and be impressed by her charm. She wished to be sought out. She had a rich friend from school, but she no longer liked to visit her. Visiting made her sad and suffer a lot inside. One evening, her husband came home and looked very happy with a big envelope. "There is something for you," he said, and he smiled. She quickly tore the paper and took out a card that had printed words on it. Georges Ramponneau asks for the honor of Mr. M. to join him. and Mme. Loisel goes to the palace on Monday evening, January 18th, and it is busy. She did not feel happy and showed anger by throwing the invitation on the table. She murmured, “What do you want me to do with that?” "But, my dear, I thought you would feel happy." You do not go out, and this is a good chance for you. I had a lot of trouble to get it, and it made me frustrated. Many people want to go to the event, but it is exclusive and hard to join. The whole official world will come, and many important people will be there. She looked at him with irritation and asked what he wanted. He did not think and stammered. He asked, "Why do you wear that dress?" It looks good to me and I like it very much. He stopped and looked because he saw his wife crying. Two big tears slowly rolled from her eyes to her mouth. He stuttered, "What is wrong?" He looked worried and confused. What is wrong with you? Are you okay or feeling sad? She worked hard to control her sadness and spoke calmly, but her cheeks were wet. I have no dress, so I cannot go to the ball. Give your card to a colleague. His wife has better tools than me. He felt very sad and worried about his life. He said, "Come, let us go and see Mathilde together." How much is a nice dress that I can wear on different occasions? She thought for a moment and made her calculations. She wanted to ask for money carefully. She hoped the clerk would not refuse her request quickly. She did not want to scare him away. She finally answered and hesitated. She said, "I think I need four hundred francs." He looked pale because he saved money for a gun. He wanted to shoot larks with friends. They planned to go to Nanterre next summer on Sundays. But he said, "Okay." I will give you four hundred francs because you need it. Try to wear a nice dress and feel good about yourself. The day of the dance came closer, and Madame felt excited for it. Loisel felt sad and anxious. He looked uneasy and worried about many things. Her dress is ready, but she feels nervous about wearing it. Her husband asked her one evening what was wrong because he cared. Come here because you have acted very strangely these last three days. She said it annoys her to have no jewels or stones to wear. I will seem worried and that will be clear to everyone. I would rather not go at all. It feels easier to stay home. He started again and said, "You can wear real flowers." It looks very good this time of year and many people like it. You can buy two or three beautiful roses for ten francs. She did not believe him, so she felt unsure about his words. There is nothing more humiliating than looking poor among rich women. Her husband cried loudly and said, "You are so stupid!" Go and find your friend, Madame. You can ask her later. Ask Forestier if she can lend you some jewels. You are close enough to her to do that. She cried out happily and said, "It is true." I did not think about it before, but now I do understand. The next day, she went to her friend and shared her problems. Mme. Forestier went to a wardrobe and took out a large jewel box. He opened it and said something to Mme. Loisel says, "Please choose, my dear." She looks at him with hope. She saw bracelets and a pearl necklace. Then, she found a gold cross with beautiful stones. She tried on the ornaments and looked in the mirror. She liked them very much but felt confused about keeping them. She kept asking if he had more. She wanted to know more about it. "Yes, I agree with you and think that is a good idea." Look. I do not know what you enjoy, so please tell me. She suddenly found a beautiful necklace in a box, and her heart beat fast because she wanted it. Her hands shook when she picked it up. She tied it around her neck and saw her dress. She felt happy to see herself. Then she asked with worry, "Can you lend me that thing?" "Yes, I can help you. What do you need?" She jumped and hugged her friend, then kissed her and ran away with the treasure. The day of the ball came, and everyone was very excited. Mme. Loisel had a big success and many people liked his work. She was more beautiful than everyone, and she smiled with joy because she felt happy. All the men looked at her and asked for her name because she was interesting. All the members of the Cabinet wanted to dance with her. The minister noticed her. He said something about her to others. She danced with joy and felt beautiful. People admired her and showed respect. She forgot everything because she felt proud, and the victory made her heart happy. She left at about four o'clock in the morning. It was early and quiet. Her husband slept since midnight in a small room. He was with three other men, and their wives had fun. He threw the wraps over her shoulders because they looked different from her fancy dress. She felt worried and wanted to escape because the other women wore nice furs. Loisel stopped her from leaving. "Please wait for a little time before you go." If you go outside, you might catch a cold. Stay warm and dress well. I will go and call a taxi because I need to leave. But she did not listen to him and she quickly went down the stairs. They were in the street, but they could not find a carriage. They looked for one and shouted to the cabmen far away. They went down to the Seine, feeling very sad and cold. They found an old boat at the dock, and it hid its sadness until dark. They reached their door on Rue des Martyrs and felt sad, so they climbed to their home. Everything was over for her and she felt very alone. He thought that he must be at the Ministry by ten o'clock. She removed the wraps from her shoulders and looked in the mirror to see her beauty. But then she shouted loudly and surprised everyone around her. She no longer has the necklace around her neck. It is gone now. Her husband was already half undressed and he asked, "What is wrong with you?" She turned quickly to him and said, "I have lost Mme." Forestier has a beautiful necklace that shines brightly and attracts attention. He stood up and looked away because he was not focused. "What! --how? It is very difficult to do this thing. Some people think it is impossible. They searched in her dress, cloak, and pockets for something very important. They looked for it, but they did not find it. He asked if she was sure she had it on when she left the ball. "Yes, I felt it in the hallway of the palace." "But if you lost it in the street, we would have heard it fall." It should be in the taxi. Yes. Probably. Did you get his phone number and save it? "

"No. Didn't you see it too? "

"No. They looked at each other in shock because they could not believe it. Finally, Loisel put on his clothes because it was time to go out. I will walk back the way we came and see if I can find it. He went outside, and he looked around the area. She sat on a chair in her big dress and waited because she felt weak. She was overwhelmed and had no energy, so she had no fire inside or clear thoughts. Her husband came back home at seven o'clock, and she was happy. He looked for something, but he found nothing at all. He went to the police and newspaper offices to offer a reward for help. He also visited cab companies because he hoped to find what he needed. She waited all day and felt very scared because of the bad event. Loisel came home at night with a pale face because he found nothing. You must tell your friend that the clasp is broken and you are fixing it now. That will give us time, so we can turn around. She wrote down what he said to her. At the end of the week, they felt very sad and lost all hope. Loisel looked five years older and said we need to find a way to replace it. The next day, they took the box with the items and went to the jeweler. He looked at his books and wanted to learn more from them. "It was not me, madam, who sold the necklace, but I gave the case." They went to different jewelry stores to find a necklace they lost and felt sad. They found diamonds in a shop at Palais Royal, and they looked like the ones they wanted. It cost forty thousand francs, which was a lot of money. They can buy it for thirty-six dollars or thirty-six euros. They asked the jeweler not to sell it for three days, and they hoped he would listen. They made a deal for him to buy it back for thirty-four thousand francs and agreed to this if they found the other one before February ends. Loisel had eighteen thousand francs that his father gave him. He wanted to take the rest of it. He needed it for later use. He borrowed money from many people, asking for a thousand francs and five hundred francs. He also took five louis from one person and three louis from another person. He borrowed money and took bad loans. He worked with lenders and moneylenders for help. He changed his life and risked his name, but he was scared of the pain. He worried about the sadness that might come to him. Then he decided to buy the necklace and gave the merchant thirty-six thousand francs. When Mme. Loisel took back the necklace from Madame. She was happy to have it again. Forestier spoke to her coldly and said she should have returned it earlier because he needed it. She did not open the case because her friend was very afraid. If she learns about the change, she will think a lot and say something important. Wouldn't she have taken Madame? Is Loisel a thief or not? Mme. Loisel now knows how hard life is for poor people. She took her part and did it suddenly with great bravery. That terrible debt must be paid, and it cannot be ignored. She will pay it because she wants to help. They let their servant leave, and then they moved to a new place to rent a small room. She learned what hard housework is and how much care the kitchen needs. She washed the dishes and used her pink nails on the dirty pots and pans. She washed the dirty clothes and dried them outside. She took water to the street every morning and brought clean water back. She stopped to rest at each landing. She wore simple clothes and went to the shops. She visited fruit, grocery, and butcher stores. She carried her basket and tried to bargain. She felt insulted and protected her small money carefully. They had to meet deadlines each month and renew some. They also needed more time. Her husband worked evenings to copy accounts and often stayed up late for money. This life lasted for ten years, and it was very important to me. After ten years, they paid everything they owed with high interest and fees. Mme. Loisel looked old now because life was hard for him. She became a strong woman for poor families because she was tough, rough, and kind. She has messy hair and her skirt is not straight. She talks loudly while washing the floor. She moves the water around with big swishes. She sat by the window and remembered a happy evening at a ball long ago. What would happen if she did not lose that necklace? I am not sure who knows the answer to this question. Nobody really knows the answer to this question, but we can keep asking. Life is strange and it changes all the time. We see things different every day. We need one small thing to help us feel safe or lost on our way. One Sunday, she walked in the Champs-Élysées to relax, and she saw a woman with a child. It was Madame. She greeted everyone with a warm smile and kind words. Forestier is young and beautiful. She is still charming and full of life. Mme. Loisel felt strong emotions and he could not hide them. Was she going to talk to her friend? Yes, of course. I agree with you completely. Now that she paid, she will tell her everything about it. Why
not? She went up the stairs and looked for her friends. "Jeanne has a good day because she feels happy today." The other person was surprised when the woman spoke. They did not know her, so they stammered, “But, madame!” I do not know and you might have made a mistake. "

"No. I am Mathilde and I live in a small town. Her friend shouted loudly and she felt surprised by the noise. "Oh, my poor Mathilde!" she said and felt sorry for her friend. "Look at how much you have changed in a short time!" I have had hard days since I met you, and they are sad because of you. "Look at me!" How so? "Do you remember the diamond necklace you lent me for the ball?" "Yes, I agree. It is a good idea." Well? "I lost it, and I am upset because it was important to me." "What do you mean by that?" You brought it back to me, and I am very happy now. "I found one for you that looks just like it." We have paid for this for ten years, and it feels very long. We found it hard because we had nothing, so it was not easy for us. Finally, it is over, and I feel very happy now. "

Mme. Forestier stopped and looked around at the quiet place around him. "You say you bought a diamond necklace to replace my necklace?" "Yes, I agree with you because it makes sense to me." "You never saw it before, so you are surprised now!" They were very friendly, and they liked to help others. She smiled with joy, and her smile was proud and simple at the same time. Mme. Forestier felt very emotional, and he took her hands gently. "Oh, my poor Mathilde! I feel sorry for her because she is unhappy." My necklace was not real and it was made of paste. It was only worth five hundred francs at most.
